title: Sovetskiy Drayv, Vol. 2: Cosmodrome
tonic_progression: Fm (Aeolian, picking up Vol. 1's unresolved tram-bell b3 and opening it OUTWARD across the empty steppe — the claustrophobic courtyard exchanged for the vast cold horizon) -> Cm (Aeolian, the long night drive out to the launch steppe) -> Gm (Dorian, the gantry floodlit on the flat horizon) -> Dm (Aeolian, the night countdown cycle begins, the tram-bell re-voiced as a countdown tone) -> Am (Phrygian, the hold — fuelling under sodium light, dread in the wait) -> Em (Locrian-shaded, the deepest hold, the steppe utterly silent before ignition) -> Bm (Phrygian-dominant flavor, IGNITION APEX — fire over an empty horizon) -> F#m (Aeolian, the craft climbs out of the atmosphere, the steppe falling away below) -> C#m (Aeolian, telemetry — the signal thinning as the craft runs ahead of recall) -> G#m / Abm (Aeolian, the craft gone too far, the last clean carrier tone before the void) -> Ebm (Aeolian, signal lost — only the empty carrier hiss across the cold dark) -> Fm (Aeolian, dawn returns to the empty steppe, the countdown tone re-read as a vast cold lullaby, the craft never coming back)
narrative_arc: Twelve tracks carry the listener out of Vol. 1's concrete factory-city and onto the open Baikonur launch steppe — a single night-into-dawn launch cycle scored as VAST COLD AWE where Vol. 1 was concrete claustrophobia, the courtyard exchanged for the empty horizon and the inner-city tram bell re-voiced as a countdown tone. Tracks 1-3 are the leaving and the arriving: track 1 picks up exactly where Vol. 1 closed, the unresolved tram-bell b3 over Fm now opened OUTWARD across an enormous flat steppe at last light, the same Polivoks-and-Juno coldwave voice but with the reverb thrown wide so the claustrophobic courtyard becomes the vast horizon (1, Fm); a long night drive out across the dark steppe toward a far cluster of lights, the boiler-room pulse re-cast as the hum of the road (2, Cm); and the floodlit gantry standing alone on the flat horizon, the first vast-cold-awe vista (3, Gm Dorian). Tracks 4-6 are the night countdown cycle: the COUNTDOWN TONE (Vol. 1's tram-bell cell re-voiced — same three-note rising-then-falling shape, now a long single sine-and-bell countdown pulse over vast reverb) begins its measured cycle as the night countdown opens (4, Dm); the hold — fuelling under sodium light, the dread of the wait, the Iron Curtain flag re-cast as a hold-klaxon (5, Am Phrygian); and the deepest hold, the steppe utterly silent and the gantry alone under the stars, the album's cold-awe valley before ignition (6, Em Locrian-shaded, drumless). Track 7 is the IGNITION APEX — fire over an empty horizon, the album's BPM ceiling at 92, awe and dread not speed; the countdown tone resolves into the roar and the craft lifts off the steppe (7, Bm Phrygian-dominant flavor). Tracks 8-11 are the telemetry and the loss: the craft climbs out of the atmosphere and the steppe falls away below (8, F#m); telemetry — the signal thinning, the craft running ahead of recall, the boiler-room pulse re-cast as a stuttering data-tick going off-grid (9, C#m); the craft gone too far, the last clean carrier tone before the void, the countdown tone fragmenting into a thin distant signal (10, G#m/Abm); and signal lost — only the empty carrier hiss across the cold dark, the Iron Curtain flag inverted into a final unanswered query (11, Ebm). Track 12 returns to the empty steppe at dawn: the gantry stands alone in grey first light, the craft never coming back, the countdown tone re-read whole one final time as a vast cold lullaby over the empty horizon, the falling leg again unresolved on the b3 — the ring closed on Fm exactly as Vol. 1 closed, but the listener now stands on the open steppe under an enormous sky instead of in the snowed-over courtyard, awe where Vol. 1 left claustrophobia (12, Fm). The album shares Vol. 1's home tonic, its three-motif architecture, its Polivoks-Juno-dark-walking-bass-TR606cold-woodblock-churchbell voice and its no-kick no-sidechain coldwave discipline — but trades concrete walls for the vast cold horizon, and trades the 24-hour city loop for a one-way launch that ends with the craft gone and the steppe empty.

recurring_motifs.motif: COUNTDOWN TONE (Vol. 1's TRAM BELL CELL re-voiced for the launch steppe) — the same three-note rising-then-falling figure (1 -> b3 -> 1) that was Vol. 1's dawn tram bell, here re-voiced as a long single sine-plus-bell COUNTDOWN PULSE: a pure sine tone with a soft bell transient struck on each pitch, run through an enormous open convolution reverb so each strike rings out across a vast cold space instead of bouncing off courtyard concrete. The interval shape and structural-anchor function are preserved exactly from Vol. 1; the voice is widened from a close inner-city tram bell to a vast-horizon countdown tone (the same call, re-purposed from summoning workers to a courtyard to marking the seconds before ignition over an empty steppe). This is the album's primary recurring figure and the direct continuity thread to Vol. 1.
recurring_motifs.transformation: Track 1 plays it WHOLE BUT OPENED OUTWARD — picking up Vol. 1's closer exactly (the falling leg unresolved on the b3 over Fm), the same three pitches now thrown into an enormous open reverb so the close courtyard tram bell becomes the first vast-cold-horizon statement, the call surfacing on the open steppe (the seam made audible: the same bell, the walls gone). Track 4 plays it AS THE LITERAL COUNTDOWN — the figure articulated as a slow measured single-pulse cycle marking the seconds, each strike a long sine-and-bell tone ringing across the vast reverb, the night countdown opening (the bell now means the launch is coming). Track 7 (ignition apex) plays it BRIGHT AND FOREGROUND with the rising leg lengthened and then RESOLVED INTO THE ROAR — the countdown tone's final strike swallowed by the ignition, the call answered by fire over the empty horizon (the one moment on the album the falling leg pushes UP toward resolution before the roar takes it). Track 12 plays it WHOLE ONE FINAL TIME, one octave down at half-tempo over the empty dawn steppe, the falling leg again UNRESOLVED holding on the b3 exactly as Vol. 1 closed — the countdown tone re-read as a vast cold lullaby over the empty horizon, the craft never coming back, the ring closed on the same unresolved b3 the album opened on.

recurring_motifs.motif: ROAD-AND-DATA PULSE (Vol. 1's BOILER-ROOM PULSE FIGURE re-cast for the steppe and the telemetry) — Vol. 1's four-note descending dark walking-bass phrase (5-4-b3-1) with a wood-block knock, here carried forward intact in shape and voice but re-cast in meaning twice: first as the HUM OF THE ROAD across the dark steppe (the drive out, the figure as the engine's mechanical pulse instead of the factory's labor rhythm), and later as a STUTTERING DATA-TICK in the telemetry tracks (the craft's downlink, the wood-block knock re-voiced as a dry data-blip). Same interval shape, same dark-walking-bass-plus-wood-block voice, same structural function as Vol. 1's labor motif (the mechanical-pulse signature appearing across the cruiser and telemetry tracks), re-located from the kombinat floor to the steppe road and the downlink channel.
recurring_motifs.transformation: Track 2 introduces it as the HUM OF THE ROAD — played clean on dark walking bass with a single wood-block knock on the downbeat of each 8-bar phrase, the figure now the mechanical pulse of the long night drive across the steppe (the East-side road analogue of Vol. 1's walking-shift-approach debut, same clean single-articulation function). Track 5 makes it the STRUCTURAL BACKBONE of the hold — articulated every 8 bars in tight lockstep with a fuelling-system pump-rhythm texture and a stiffened dry wood-block knock, the launch-pad's mechanical rhythm of the fuelling sequence (the steppe-side analogue of Vol. 1's quota-chorus backbone). Track 9 re-casts it as the STUTTERING DATA-TICK going OFF-GRID — each note delayed by a sixteenth from the grid and the wood-block knock re-voiced as a dry data-blip arriving early on each repetition, the telemetry downlink losing its lock as the craft runs ahead of recall (the steppe-side analogue of Vol. 1's off-grid twenty-second-holds statement). Track 11 plays only the first three notes (5-4-b3) on dark walking bass alone with no wood-block knock and no data-tick — the downlink is gone, only the empty carrier hiss remains, the data pulse dissolved into the cold dark (the steppe-side analogue of Vol. 1's fragmented tram-home statement, the craft now lost).

recurring_motifs.motif: LAUNCH KLAXON (Vol. 1's IRON CURTAIN FLAG re-cast for the launch sequence) — Vol. 1's chromatic-falling Polivoks lead cell (b2 -> 1 -> 7 -> b6) with a deep dry church-bell tone, carried forward intact in interval shape and escalation function but the church-bell strike re-voiced as a deep dry LAUNCH-KLAXON tone (a low alarm-horn pulse struck on the b2, the same single-strike accent but now the pad-klaxon of the launch sequence rather than the propaganda church bell). Same chromatic-falling shape, same escalation-then-inversion-into-release logic across its appearances as Vol. 1's flag motif, re-located from the watch-room to the launch hold and the telemetry-loss.
recurring_motifs.transformation: Track 5 introduces it as a SINGLE ARTICULATION at the chorus midpoint of the hold — the chromatic-falling cell descending with a single deep dry launch-klaxon tone on the b2 strike, the hold-klaxon sounding once as fuelling reaches pressure (the steppe-side analogue of Vol. 1's stations-taking single-articulation debut). Track 7 (ignition apex) makes it the STRUCTURAL PULSE of the launch — the cell repeated every 4 bars throughout the chorus, each repetition heavier in the lead voicing and the launch-klaxon tones stacking into a quiet dread that becomes the roar (the steppe-side analogue of Vol. 1's crisis-peak structural-pulse statement). Track 11 plays it ONE FINAL TIME INVERTED (b6 -> 7 -> 1 -> b2) but RESOLVING NOWHERE — where Vol. 1 inverted the flag into a clean stand-down release, here the inverted cell is a final unanswered query sent up into the void, the launch-klaxon tone struck once at one-third volume and left hanging with no reply, the signal-lost gesture (the inversion is the same shape as Vol. 1's release but its meaning is loss, not relief — there is no craft left to answer).

tracks.composer_notes: OPENER / VOL. 1 SEAM PICKUP. This track must make the listener recognize the SAME album-world continuing but the WALLS GONE: the COUNTDOWN TONE is Vol. 1's tram-bell cell re-voiced (identical 1 -> b3 -> 1 shape, sine-plus-bell instead of Polivoks-lead tram bell) and thrown into an ENORMOUS open cold reverb so the close courtyard bell becomes the vast-horizon countdown tone — the single most important continuity gesture on the album. The home tonic stays F minor (shared with Vol. 1) so the seam is harmonically seamless. The mood pivot is the whole point: Vol. 1 was concrete claustrophobia (close plate reverb, fluorescent hum, courtyard walls); Vol. 2 is vast cold awe (enormous open convolution reverb, steppe-wind floor, dead-level horizon). NO KICK ANYWHERE, sparse TR-606-cold snare and dark walking bass only, exactly as Vol. 1. The outro holds the falling leg UNRESOLVED on the b3 (re-opening Vol. 1's exact closing gesture) — the launch cycle begins on the same unresolved b3 the city cycle ended on. Outro on Fm tonic (not the dominant) — the album begins on stillness on the open steppe. Track 2 takes the V (C) of Fm and reroots it as Cm for the night drive out.

tracks.composer_notes: COMEDOWN / signal lost. The LAUNCH KLAXON returns ONE FINAL TIME INVERTED (b6 -> 7 -> 1 -> b2) but RESOLVING NOWHERE — this is the deliberate divergence from Vol. 1: where Vol. 1's Iron Curtain flag inverted into a clean stand-down RELEASE (the watch room exhaling), Vol. 2's launch klaxon inverts into a final UNANSWERED QUERY left hanging at one-third volume — the same inversion shape, the opposite meaning (loss, not relief; there is no craft left to answer). The ROAD-AND-DATA PULSE returns FRAGMENTED to its first three notes (5-4-b3) on dark walking bass alone — its final appearance, the downlink gone (the steppe-side analogue of Vol. 1 track 10's fragmented tram-home statement, the data pulse dissolved into the empty carrier). The empty carrier hiss is the album's signal-loss texture. 240s. NO KICK ANYWHERE. Outro Bb (V of Ebm) prepares the perfect-fifth descent into Fm of track 12 via the flat-side cycle-of-fifths-descending ladder closing the ring — back to the empty steppe at dawn.

tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Down a perfect fifth from Ebm to Fm via cycle-of-fifths-descending logic — F is a perfect fifth below Bb (the V that closed track 11), and the album closes the ring on the SAME Fm tonic that opened both Vol. 2 and Vol. 1. Twelve tracks have traversed the full sharp-side cycle-of-fifths-ascending ladder through the leaving and countdown and ignition and ascent (Fm through C#m, eight perfect-fifth steps) and a partial flat-side cycle-of-fifths-descending ladder through the signal-loss void (Abm to Ebm to Fm, three perfect-fifth steps) — the identical harmonic architecture of Vol. 1, here mapped onto the launch arc instead of the city's 24-hour cycle. The listener arrives back at the empty steppe at dawn from the opposite side of the harmonic loop than the night drive departed from.
tracks.tempo_relationship_to_prior: -2 BPM, the lowest BPM on the record and exactly the opener's 80 BPM — the launch cycle closes back on its opening tempo shelf (Vol. 2's range is 80-92, one notch tighter at the bottom than Vol. 1's 78-92; this closer holds the validation-safe 80 floor rather than dipping to Vol. 1's 78, keeping the whole of Vol. 2 inside the strict 80-118 validator).
tracks.composer_notes: CLOSER / RING CLOSE + VOL. 1 SEAM ECHO. The COUNTDOWN TONE returns WHOLE ONE OCTAVE DOWN AT HALF-TEMPO with the falling leg UNRESOLVED holding on the b3 — its fourth structural appearance, re-read as a vast cold lullaby over the empty horizon, the EXACT closing gesture Vol. 1 used (Vol. 1 closed its tram bell cell one octave down at half-tempo with the falling leg unresolved on the b3 over Fm; Vol. 2 closes its countdown tone identically). The album ends on Fm — the same tonic that opened Vol. 2 AND closed Vol. 1 — but the listener now stands on the open steppe under an enormous pale dawn sky instead of in the snowed-over courtyard: AWE where Vol. 1 left CLAUSTROPHOBIA, the craft gone and the steppe empty where Vol. 1 left the city ringing closed on its loop. 300s long for the coda's full lullaby-time. The final 16-bar coda is the countdown tone alone over the fading wide Juno pad and dark walking bass with the dawn-steppe-wind floor fading last — do not let the renderer add a fade-up tail, a stinger, or any rhythmic figure in the final 16 bars; the unresolved sine-plus-bell b3 over the fading wide pad to silence IS the composition. The unresolved falling leg signals the craft is not coming back and the empty steppe will wait — the ring is closed on the same Fm and the same unresolved b3 that bracket both volumes, but the meaning has moved from concrete claustrophobia to vast cold awe. Do not let the renderer substitute a kick drum or a PWM synthwave lead anywhere — the closer must remain in the album's Soviet atompunk coldwave palette of Polivoks sawtooth lead, wide Juno-60 pad with enormous open cold reverb, dark synth walking bass, dry mechanical TR-606-cold snare, wood-block knock, sine-plus-bell countdown tone, and launch-klaxon tone.

album_notes.overall_form: Single night-into-dawn launch cycle over 12 tracks at the Baikonur launch steppe — a deliberate VAST-COLD-AWE counterpart to Vol. 1's concrete-claustrophobia 24-hour city cycle, sharing Vol. 1's F-minor home tonic, three-motif architecture, Polivoks-Juno-dark-walking-bass-TR606cold-woodblock voice, and no-kick no-sidechain coldwave discipline, but trading the courtyard walls for the open steppe horizon and the city loop for a one-way launch. Three arcs: Leaving and arriving (tracks 1-3): the walls fall away onto the open steppe at last light (track 1, the Vol. 1 seam pickup), the long night drive out to the pad (track 2), the floodlit gantry alone on the horizon (track 3). Tempo climbs in small +2 BPM increments from 80 to 84 BPM. Countdown and hold (tracks 4-6): the night countdown cycle begins with the tram-bell re-voiced as a countdown tone (track 4, +4 to 88 BPM), the tense fuelling hold under sodium light (track 5, 90 BPM shelf), the deepest hold with the steppe utterly silent (track 6, valley dip to 86 BPM, drumless). Ignition and loss (tracks 7-12): ignition apex — fire over an empty horizon (track 7, +6 to the 92 BPM ceiling), the craft climbing out of the atmosphere (track 8, -2 to 90), telemetry thinning (track 9, -4 to 86, flat-side pivot point), the craft too far to recall (track 10, -2 to 84, flat-side pivot), signal lost into the empty carrier (track 11, -2 to 82), and the empty steppe at dawn with the craft never coming back (track 12, -2 to 80, ring close on Fm). The album ends on Fm — the same tonic that opened Vol. 2 and closed Vol. 1 — with the countdown tone's falling leg unresolved on the b3, the craft gone and the steppe empty: AWE where Vol. 1 left claustrophobia. The launch-cycle structure mirrors Vol. 1's 24-hour-cycle arc beat-by-beat — Vol. 2 track 1's open steppe is the vast counterpart of Vol. 1 track 1's close courtyard, Vol. 2 track 4's countdown is the counterpart of Vol. 1 track 4's lathe row, Vol. 2 track 7's ignition is the counterpart of Vol. 1 track 7's radar contact, and Vol. 2 track 12's empty dawn steppe is the counterpart of Vol. 1 track 12's snowed-over midnight courtyard — but Vol. 2 is a one-way launch (the craft does not return) where Vol. 1 was a closed loop (the day returns to dawn).
album_notes.key_strategy: Identical harmonic architecture to Vol. 1, re-mapped onto the launch arc. Sharp-side cycle-of-fifths ascending through eight perfect-fifth steps for the leaving and countdown and ignition and ascent: Fm -> Cm -> Gm (Dorian raised-6) -> Dm -> Am (Phrygian b2) -> Em (Locrian-shaded b5) -> Bm (Phrygian-dominant flavor) -> F#m -> C#m. Modal shadings progress plain minor (Fm, Cm, Dm) through Dorian (Gm) into the hold's Phrygian (Am) and the deepest-hold Locrian-shaded (Em) and into the ignition's Phrygian-dominant (Bm), then release to plain Aeolian (F#m, C#m) as the craft climbs into clear black. The descent flips to flat-side cycle-of-fifths descending through three perfect-fifth steps for the signal-loss void: C#m -> Abm (enharmonic G#m pivot) -> Ebm -> Fm, returning to the home tonic from the opposite side of the loop. NO MAJOR TONICS appear — Soviet atompunk coldwave lives in minor and modal-minor, exactly like Vol. 1. The home tonic F minor is shared with Vol. 1 (not transposed) because Vol. 2 picks up Vol. 1's exact unresolved closing gesture (the tram-bell/countdown-tone falling leg on the b3 over Fm) — the shared tonic is the seam. The album never resolves to a comfortable harmonic frame; the closer's unresolved countdown-tone b3 over Fm is the final harmonic gesture, identical to Vol. 1's close, bracketing both volumes on the same unresolved b3.
album_notes.tempo_strategy: Tightened to 80-92 BPM (Vol. 1 was 78-92; Vol. 2 holds the validation-safe 80 floor at both ends rather than dipping to 78, keeping the entire album inside the strict nightdrive-llm::validate_spec 80-118 bound with no flag needed). Leaving accelerando: 80 -> 82 -> 84 in small +2 increments, the night drive across the steppe as patient as Vol. 1's morning. Countdown quickening: +4 to 88 BPM (track 4) as the countdown engages, then 90 BPM shelf (track 5, the hold). Valley: 86 BPM (track 6, the deepest hold, a -4 dip). Peak: 92 BPM (track 7, ignition) — the album's BPM ceiling and a single +6 BPM jump from the valley, exactly matching Vol. 1's valley-to-peak jump (86 -> 92). The peak is awe and dread, not speed; 92 is the ceiling for both volumes. Descent off the peak: 90 -> 86 -> 84 -> 82 -> 80 in small -2/-4 increments through the ascent and telemetry and signal-loss, mirroring the leaving accelerando step size in reverse and ending exactly on the opener's 80 BPM. The mechanical sidechain-pumping BPM range of synthwave (104+ with hard kick) is absent — this album lives in the same coldwave / post-punk-coldwave tempo territory as Vol. 1, the launch cycle dragging by design exactly as the city cycle did.
album_notes.motif_strategy: Three motifs carried directly from Vol. 1, each re-voiced for the launch steppe while preserving its exact interval shape and structural function. (1) COUNTDOWN TONE = Vol. 1's TRAM BELL CELL re-voiced — same three-note rising-then-falling shape (1 -> b3 -> 1), re-voiced from a close inner-city Polivoks tram bell to a vast-horizon sine-plus-bell countdown pulse in enormous open reverb. Four structural appearances at the same phase-transition slots as Vol. 1: track 1 WHOLE BUT OPENED OUTWARD (the Vol. 1 seam pickup, the same unresolved b3 thrown wide), track 4 AS THE LITERAL COUNTDOWN (the bell now marks the seconds before launch), track 7 BRIGHT AND FOREGROUND RESOLVED INTO THE ROAR (ignition apex, the one near-resolution before fire takes it), track 12 WHOLE ONE OCTAVE DOWN AT HALF-TEMPO WITH THE FALLING LEG UNRESOLVED (closer, the vast cold lullaby, the exact gesture Vol. 1 closed on). A deliberate fifth FRAGMENTED appearance at track 10 (only the upper rising leg, tiny and far in the void) extends the schedule for the launch narrative — the countdown lost in the void. This is the album's primary cross-volume continuity thread: the same call, re-purposed from summoning workers to a courtyard to marking the seconds before ignition over an empty steppe. (2) ROAD-AND-DATA PULSE = Vol. 1's BOILER-ROOM PULSE FIGURE carried forward intact in shape (5-4-b3-1) and voice (dark walking bass plus wood-block knock), re-cast in meaning: HUM OF THE ROAD on the night drive (track 2, clean single-articulation debut), STRUCTURAL BACKBONE lockstepped with a fuelling-pump rhythm at the hold (track 5), STUTTERING DATA-TICK going off-grid in the telemetry (track 9, each note delayed a sixteenth, wood-block re-voiced as a data-blip), and FRAGMENTED to its first three notes when the signal is lost (track 11). Same tracks-2/5/9/11 schedule and same transformation pattern as Vol. 1's labor motif (3/5/8/10 shifted to 2/5/9/11 for the launch arc), re-located from the kombinat floor to the steppe road and the downlink channel. (3) LAUNCH KLAXON = Vol. 1's IRON CURTAIN FLAG re-cast — same chromatic-falling cell (b2 -> 1 -> 7 -> b6), the church-bell strike re-voiced as a deep dry launch-klaxon tone: SINGLE ARTICULATION at the fuelling hold (track 5), STRUCTURAL PULSE every 4 bars stacking into the roar at ignition (track 7), and ONE FINAL INVERTED ARTICULATION RESOLVING NOWHERE at signal-loss (track 11). The track-5/7/11 schedule and the inversion-at-the-end logic mirror Vol. 1's flag (4/7/9/11 compressed to 5/7/11) — but the inversion's meaning is DELIBERATELY DIVERGED: Vol. 1's flag inverted into a clean stand-down RELEASE (the watch room exhaling), Vol. 2's klaxon inverts into a final UNANSWERED QUERY left hanging (loss, not relief; no craft left to answer). The single-articulation appearance at track 5 and the inverted appearance at track 11 bracket the launch-and-loss arc as a structural unit.
album_notes.tracklist_continuity: Each track ends on a pitch that primes the next track's tonic via dominant relationship (V of next tonic), with the flat-side pivot at the descent boundary (track 9 to 10) and the descent close (track 11 to 12) — the exact continuity scheme of Vol. 1, mapped onto the launch arc. Specifically: 1 ends on Fm tonic (no V — the album begins on stillness on the open steppe; primes the perfect-fifth ascent to Cm). 2 ends on G (V of Cm, reread as Gm of track 3). 3 ends on D (V of Gm, reread as Dm of track 4). 4 ends on A (V of Dm, reread as Am of track 5). 5 ends on E (V of Am, reread as Em of track 6). 6 ends on B (V of Em, reread as Bm of track 7). 7 ends on F# (V of Bm, reread as F#m of track 8). 8 ends on C# (V of F#m, reread as C#m of track 9). 9 ends on G# (V of C#m, reread enharmonically as Ab tonic of track 10 — the flat-side pivot that begins the signal-loss descent). 10 ends on Eb (V of Abm, reread as Ebm of track 11). 11 ends on Bb (V of Ebm, prepares the perfect-fifth descent into Fm of track 12 via the flat-side cycle-of-fifths-descending ladder closing the ring). The listener will only feel each transition is inevitable; the descent's harmonic logic reverses the ascent's by direction of cycle-of-fifths motion. This continuity scheme is the exact analogue of Vol. 1's, shared note-for-note because the two volumes share the F-minor home tonic and the same eight-step-ascending / three-step-descending ladder.
album_notes.visual_continuity_notes: Twelve covers follow the launch cycle as a single night-into-dawn arc on the open Baikonur steppe — the deliberate VAST counterpart to Vol. 1's close courtyard-kitchen-kombinat interiors. The aesthetic vocabulary shares Vol. 1's cold greyscale and ice-blue and steel-grey base but trades concrete walls and fluorescent interiors for VAST EXTERIOR SPACE: enormous flat steppe horizons, vast starfields, deep cold space-black, and the new amber/sodium/cryogenic-white accents of the launch complex. No city, no walls, no neon, no palm trees, no warm sunset magenta, no cyan-blue cyberpunk, no red-violet anime cityscape, no people visible, no faces, no text legible, no Cyrillic letters (SDXL would render them garbled). Track 1: vast empty steppe at last light with a far cluster of pad lights on the horizon (exterior wide). Track 2: a dark road across the night steppe toward distant pad lights with headlight cones (exterior wide). Track 3: a single floodlit gantry monumental and alone on the flat horizon under stars (exterior, hero cover 1). Track 4: the gantry base closer with sodium service lights and low mist on the apron (exterior close). Track 5: the fuelling arms with cryogenic vapor venting and an amber alarm lamp (exterior close). Track 6: the gantry tiny against the entire starfield, the Milky Way arcing over the steppe (exterior wide low-angle, the cold-awe valley vista). Track 7: ignition — a brilliant white-orange flame column and smoke wall at the gantry base lighting the steppe (exterior, hero cover 2). Track 8: a high-altitude view beside the rising rocket, the steppe and curving horizon falling away (exterior space). Track 9: a tiny distant craft against deep space with a faint signal line to a small earth-crescent (space). Track 10: an almost-empty frame with a single tiny fading point of light in the cold void (space). Track 11: an empty frame of black void with faint static texture, no craft visible (space). Track 12: the same lone gantry as track 3 now empty on the steppe at grey dawn, the rocket gone, scorch marks on the apron (exterior wide). The architectural rhyme of tracks 3 and 12 (same gantry, before and after the launch — the rocket present then gone) and the steady visual descent from steppe-surface (1-7) up through the atmosphere (8) and out into progressively emptier deep space (9-11) before returning to the empty steppe at dawn (12) is the album's visual closing-of-the-ring and the inverse of Vol. 1's interior-to-interior loop (Vol. 2 goes surface-to-void-to-surface where Vol. 1 went courtyard-to-kombinat-to-courtyard). All cover prompts front-load aggressive SDXL text-suppression in the first 50 words ('no text, no words, no lettering, no typography, no font, no characters, no labels, no signage with readable words, no propaganda text, no Cyrillic letters, no people visible, no faces') placed BEFORE the scene description because CLIP truncates at 77 tokens. All cover prompts are written for SDXL native generation at 1024x1024, 1344x768, and 768x1344 — scene depth survives reframing across all three aspect ratios, no pose-locked compositions.

album_notes.differentiation_from_prior_albums: Vol. 2 is the deliberate VAST-COLD-AWE counterpart to Vol. 1's CONCRETE-CLAUSTROPHOBIA, sharing Vol. 1's exact composer voice and harmonic architecture while inverting its sense of space. SHARED with Vol. 1 (the continuity that makes this read as the same project's Vol. 2): F-minor home tonic (not transposed — the seam is the shared tonic and the shared unresolved closing b3); the same three-motif architecture (tram-bell/countdown-tone, boiler-room/road-and-data pulse, Iron-Curtain/launch klaxon) with the same interval shapes and structural functions; the same Polivoks-style sawtooth lead, Juno-60-style sawtooth pad, dark synth walking bass, dry mechanical TR-606-cold drum machine (sparse snare on 2 and 4, closed hi-hat, NO KICK, NO four-on-the-floor, NO 808), wood-block knock, and single deep dry bell/klaxon-tone accents; the same no-kick no-sidechain coldwave discipline; the same eight-step-ascending / three-step-descending cycle-of-fifths ladder; the same 92 BPM ceiling and same valley-to-peak +6 jump. INVERTED from Vol. 1 (the mood pivot that makes this Cosmodrome rather than another city cycle): the close PLATE reverb of the courtyard becomes ENORMOUS OPEN COLD CONVOLUTION reverb of the steppe and the void; the fluorescent-tube electrical hum floor becomes a vast STEPPE-WIND floor; the close inner-city Polivoks TRAM BELL becomes a vast-horizon SINE-PLUS-BELL countdown tone; the teletype-clatter texture becomes a FUELLING-PUMP rhythm and a DOWNLINK-CARRIER hiss; the church-bell strike becomes a LAUNCH-KLAXON tone; the propaganda church bell becomes the ignition ROAR (an ignition-rumble texture that replaces the absent kick at the peak); and the closed 24-hour city LOOP becomes a one-way LAUNCH that ends with the craft gone and the steppe empty (AWE where Vol. 1 left claustrophobia, the open horizon where Vol. 1 left concrete walls). Distinct from prior catalog volumes: Sunset Vol. 1 was warm Miami synthwave (DX7, FM bells, sidechain kick, A minor); Tron Vol. 1 was cold cyan-magenta cyberpunk (PWM saws, hard sidechain, A minor); Neo-Tokyo Vol. 1 was Akira anime-palette vertical descent (FM bell over brass, hard kick, D minor); Atompunk Western Vol. 1 was mid-century American Cold War in steel-grey/Tang-orange (Theremin, muted brass, vibraphone, timpani, C minor). Distinct from berlin-wall (a separate Cold-War-adjacent theme) by setting (the open Baikonur launch steppe and near-space, not a divided city's wall) and by arc (a one-way launch into the void, not a wall's standoff). Title danger zone respected for keys ['sovetskiy']: NO named real missions or cosmonauts as titles (no 'Vostok' / 'Soyuz' / 'Sputnik' / 'Gagarin' / 'Komarov' / 'Tereshkova' / 'Laika' / 'Korolev' / 'Baikonur' as a title word); NO title that is BOTH a space-film named element AND a track on that film's OST (checked against 2001: A Space Odyssey, Apollo 13, Gravity, First Man, Interstellar, The Right Stuff scores — e.g. avoided 'Blue Danube', 'Daybreak', 'Re-Entry', 'Docking', 'The Launch', 'Trans-Lunar', 'Contact', 'Mountains', 'No Time For Caution', any 'Countdown'-as-bare-title OST hit). Single-hit evocative-generic countdown/steppe/telemetry imagery used throughout (The Walls Fall Away, Night Drive to the Pad, Gantry on the Horizon, Countdown Begins, Hold at Fuelling, The Steppe Holds Its Breath, Fire Over an Empty Horizon, Climbing Out, Telemetry Thinning, Too Far to Recall, Carrier Then Silence, Empty Steppe at Dawn) — none of which name a real mission, cosmonaut, or film-OST track.
